title: Population estimates and projections | Stats NZ
content:
## Population estimates and projections
Estimates and projections give information about the number and types of people in the population, and how that may change.
Estimates describe the size and characteristics of the population on a past date. Projections describe trends to show what the population may be like in future.
- the numbers of people in New Zealand and in different areas of New Zealand
- characteristics of these populations
- projections of how these populations will change.

## Latest information releases about estimates and projections

## Statistical area 2 and 3 population projections: 2023(base)–2053 – fourth instalment
Statistical area 2 (SA2) and statistical area 3 (SA3) population projections released in Aotearoa Data Explorer (ADE) provide an indication of future changes in the size and age-sex structure of the population usually living in each area.

## Māori population estimates: Mean year ended 31 December 2025
Māori population estimates give the best available measure of the Māori ethnic population, by age and sex, usually living in Aotearoa New Zealand.

## National population estimates: At 31 March 2026
National population estimates give the best available measure of the population, by age and sex, usually living in Aotearoa New Zealand.

## Household labour force survey estimated working-age population: March 2026 quarter
The household labour force survey estimated working-age population table shows the population benchmarks used to produce household labour force survey estimates for the upcoming labour market statistics release.

## Latest publications about estimates and projections

## Estimated population of NZ
Find the latest population figures for New Zealand.

## Auckland population projected to reach 2 million by 2033
International migration has become the main driver of population growth in Auckland, with 2 in 5 Aucklanders now born overseas.

## Guide to mortality measures
Describes a range of mortality measures, discusses the strengths and limitations of these measures, and identifies where published measures are available.

## Guide to fertility measures
Describes a range of fertility measures, discusses the strengths and limitations of these measures, and identifies where published measures are available.

## CSV files for download
CSV files for download has data from Infoshare and Stats NZ information releases.

## Tools with estimates and projections data
The population clock estimates New Zealand's population in real time.

## Aotearoa Data Explorer
A data tool to search for data, create tables customising the variables and layout, and download Excel or CSV files.

## Place and ethnic group summaries
Explore statistics about the population by region, district, and smaller areas, and by ethnic group.

## Interactive population pyramid for New Zealand
Use this interactive population pyramid to see our population history and what our future may look like.
Build tables from our biggest range of datasets, including long-term time series. View on screen or download Excel or CSV files.
Get statistical metadata – what we measure, definitions, and how we produce statistics.

## Experimental initiatives

## Experimental population estimates from linked administrative data
View experimental population estimates for New Zealand derived from linked administrative data in the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI).

## Data Knowledge Centre
This prototype can help you find, use, and manage New Zealand government data.

## Information about data
Get technical information such as classifications used, survey design, and a glossary of statistical terms.

### Metadata in DataInfo+
DataInfo+ has metadata about our statistical collections and classifications, and questionnaires and forms.
DataInfo+ concepts gives definitions of terms used by Stats NZ.
Demographic estimates – DataInfo+ explains the general methodology used to produce population estimates, and definitions and related metadata.
Demographic projections – DataInfo+ explains the general methodology used to produce population projections, and definitions and related metadata.

### Methods, sources, and user guides
Population statistics – user guide provides information about population statistics we produce.
Standard for population terms will help you understand and use Stats NZ's population measures.
- Gender sex and intersex
